title: VB-GRAM G (Viksit Bharat Gramin Abhiyaan Mahatma Gandhi) Replaces MGNREGA from July 1, 2026
summary: The Viksit Bharat Gramin Abhiyaan Mahatma Gandhi (VB-GRAM G) scheme officially replaced the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) from July 1, 2026. The new scheme introduces five significant changes compared to the earlier programme, including revised state-wise wage structures. The transition marks a major overhaul of India's flagship rural employment guarantee framework.
exam_angle: This is a direct replacement of one of India's most significant welfare schemes (MGNREGA, enacted 2005); aspirants must know the changes introduced, the new name, and the constitutional/statutory basis of such employment guarantee programmes.
key_facts: New scheme: VB-GRAM G (Viksit Bharat Gramin Abhiyaan Mahatma Gandhi)
Replaced: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005)
Effective date: July 1, 2026
Introduces 5 key changes including revised state-wise wage rates

title: Commercial LPG Cylinder Prices Cut by Rs 183.50 from July 1, 2026
summary: The government slashed commercial LPG cylinder prices by Rs 183.50 effective July 1, 2026. This is a significant reduction affecting hotels, restaurants, and commercial establishments across India. The price revision is linked to easing global oil prices and is separate from domestic cooking gas cylinders used in households.
exam_angle: LPG price revisions are linked to India's administered pricing mechanism, global crude oil trends, and the role of oil marketing companies (OMCs) under the Ministry of Petroleum; also relevant to inflation management and fuel subsidy policy.
key_facts: Price cut: Rs 183.50 per commercial LPG cylinder
Effective: July 1, 2026
Applies to commercial (not domestic) LPG cylinders
Driven by easing global crude oil prices
source: The Indian Express / The Times of India

title: Cabinet Approves Rs 14,115 Crore Road Projects Including Six-Lane Dwarka Tunnel in Delhi
summary: The Union Cabinet cleared road infrastructure projects worth Rs 14,115 crore, including the construction of a six-lane Dwarka tunnel in Delhi and the Kanpur-Kabrai highway project in Uttar Pradesh. The approvals are part of the government's ongoing push to expand the national highway network. These projects are expected to ease urban traffic congestion and improve inter-city connectivity.
exam_angle: Cabinet approvals for infrastructure are standard UPSC current affairs; connects to Bharatmala Pariyojana, Ministry of Road Transport & Highways, and urban infrastructure planning.
key_facts: Total project cost: Rs 14,115 crore
Key projects: Six-lane Dwarka tunnel (Delhi) and Kanpur-Kabrai highway (UP)
Approved by: Union Cabinet
Ministry: Road Transport & Highways

title: Bill to Remove Jailed Ministers After 30 Days in Custody Likely in Monsoon Session
summary: The Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) examining the bill to disqualify Chief Ministers and ministers jailed for 30 days or more is likely to retain the key clause on removal. The JPC is expected to table its report ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament. The bill, if passed, would mandate automatic removal of elected officeholders held in judicial custody beyond 30 days.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to constitutional provisions on disqualification of legislators (Articles 102, 191), the Representation of the People Act, and separation of powers; a key polity topic for UPSC Mains.
key_facts: Bill proposes: Removal of CM/ministers jailed for 30+ days
Body examining: Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC)
Expected: Monsoon Session of Parliament
Constitutional angle: Articles 102 and 191 on disqualification

title: Centre Issues Notice to Meta, Asks WhatsApp to Halt Username Feature Over Online Fraud Concerns
summary: The Central Government issued a notice to Meta asking it not to roll out WhatsApp's new username feature until consultations with the government are completed. The government expressed concerns that the feature could be exploited for online fraud and money laundering. The move reflects India's proactive regulatory approach to social media platforms under the IT Act and Digital Personal Data Protection framework.
exam_angle: Connects to India's IT Rules 2021, the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023, intermediary liability, and the government's powers to regulate social media platforms; relevant for GS Paper 2 (Governance/Polity).
key_facts: Government body: Ministry of Electronics & IT (MeitY)
Platform: WhatsApp (Meta)
Concern: Online fraud and money laundering via username feature
Regulatory framework: IT Act 2000, IT Rules 2021

title: CBI Arrests IPS Officer on Central Deputation in Rs 3 Crore Bribery Case
summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) arrested an IPS officer serving on central deputation for allegedly demanding a bribe of Rs 3 crore to 'fix' a CBI fake drugs case. A Delhi court sent the officer to custody after arrest. The case highlights issues of corruption within law enforcement and the role of CBI as an anti-corruption body.
exam_angle: Relevant to the role and powers of CBI, anti-corruption laws (Prevention of Corruption Act), IPS cadre rules on central deputation, and the debate on CBI's accountability.
key_facts: Arresting agency: CBI
Accused: IPS officer on central deputation (Haryana cadre)
Alleged bribe: Rs 3 crore
Purpose: To 'fix' a CBI fake drugs case

title: Russia Purchases Gasoline from India to Tackle Domestic Fuel Shortages
summary: Russia has turned to India to buy gasoline amid a domestic fuel shortage, according to a Reuters report. Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian oil refineries have caused significant disruptions, leading Russia into a summer fuel crisis. India, which has significantly expanded its oil trade with Russia since 2022, is now emerging as a supplier of refined petroleum products to Russia as well.
exam_angle: Highlights the evolving India-Russia energy relationship, India's role as a major importer and now re-exporter of Russian energy, and geopolitical implications of the Ukraine conflict on global energy markets.
key_facts: Russia facing fuel shortage due to Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries
Russia is buying gasoline (refined product) from India
India became Russia's top oil buyer after 2022 sanctions
Source: Reuters exclusive report

title: Iran Asserts Control Over Strait of Hormuz, Rejects Third-Party Intervention
summary: Iran has drawn red lines over the Strait of Hormuz, rejecting any third-party intervention and insisting on maintaining control over the strategically vital waterway. Senior Iranian sources told Reuters that Tehran would not allow external interference in the strait. Separately, a ship ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz amid heightened regional tensions.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world's most critical oil chokepoints; Iran's control claims are significant for global energy security, India's oil imports, and the broader US-Iran geopolitical standoff.
key_facts: Strait of Hormuz: ~20% of global oil trade passes through it
Iran rejects third-party intervention in the strait
Backdrop: US-Iran nuclear deal negotiations ongoing in Doha
A ship ran aground in the strait amid tensions

title: US-Iran Indirect Nuclear Talks Held in Doha with Mediators; Trump Calls Meetings 'Very Good'
summary: The United States and Iran held indirect talks in Doha, Qatar, with mediators facilitating discussions on Iran's nuclear programme. US President Donald Trump described the meetings as 'very good'. The talks come amid heightened tensions following the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and uncertainty about Iran's future leadership and nuclear posture.
exam_angle: Iran's nuclear programme, the JCPOA framework, and US-Iran relations are perennial UPSC topics; the post-Khamenei power transition in Iran is also highly relevant for IR.
key_facts: Venue: Doha, Qatar
Format: Indirect talks via mediators
Context: Post-Khamenei Iran and US pressure on nuclear programme
Trump described meetings as 'very good'

title: PM Modi Dials Iranian President; India-Iran Diplomatic Engagement Continues
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi telephoned the Iranian President to discuss bilateral ties amid the rapidly changing geopolitical situation in Iran following Supreme Leader Khamenei's death. The call signals India's intent to maintain diplomatic continuity with Tehran. India has significant interests in Iran including the Chabahar Port project and regional connectivity via the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
exam_angle: India-Iran relations encompass Chabahar Port (exempted from US sanctions for India), INSTC, energy ties, and the Indian diaspora in the Gulf; this diplomatic outreach is exam-relevant.
key_facts: PM Modi called the Iranian President
Context: Post-Khamenei leadership transition in Iran
Key India-Iran projects: Chabahar Port, INSTC
India balances ties with both Iran and USA/Israel

title: India Records Driest June in Over a Decade, Raising Farming Concerns
summary: India experienced its driest June in more than a decade, with rainfall significantly below the long-period average, according to BBC and IMD data. This has raised serious concerns for the agricultural sector, particularly for kharif crop sowing which depends on the southwest monsoon onset. The deficit monsoon is also expected to impact reservoir levels, water availability, and rural incomes.
exam_angle: Monsoon performance is directly linked to agricultural output, food inflation, rural economy, and kharif crop production; connects to IMD's role, La Niña/El Niño patterns, and GS Paper 1 (Geography) and GS Paper 3 (Agriculture/Economy).
key_facts: India recorded its driest June in over 10 years
Southwest monsoon is critical for kharif crop sowing
Impact: Reservoir levels, food inflation, rural incomes
July rainfall is considered crucial for annual agricultural output

title: Assam Floods Worsen; Nearly 50,000 People Affected Across Four Districts
summary: Flood conditions in Assam worsened, with nearly 50,000 people affected across four districts. The annual Assam floods, driven by Brahmaputra river flooding, are a recurring disaster in the state. Relief and rescue operations have been deployed by the State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) and National Disaster Response Force (NDRF).
exam_angle: Assam floods are a recurring disaster management topic; connects to the Brahmaputra river system, NDRF, SDRF, Disaster Management Act 2005, and the broader issue of flood-prone states in India.
key_facts: Affected: ~50,000 people across 4 districts in Assam
Cause: Annual flooding, primarily Brahmaputra river system
Response: SDRF and NDRF deployed
Assam is one of India's most flood-prone states

title: Foreign Buying of Indian Government Bonds Hits Record High After Tax Break
summary: Foreign investment in Indian government bonds has reached a record high following a tax exemption introduced by the government to attract overseas capital. The development comes after India's inclusion in global bond indices such as the JP Morgan Emerging Market Bond Index. Increased foreign participation in Indian sovereign debt is expected to lower borrowing costs and deepen the domestic bond market.
exam_angle: India's bond market liberalisation, inclusion in global bond indices (JP Morgan, Bloomberg), FAR (Fully Accessible Route) bonds, and capital account management are key RBI/economy topics for UPSC.
key_facts: Foreign buying of Indian bonds hit a record high
Triggered by: Tax exemption on bond income for foreign investors
India included in JP Morgan EM Bond Index (since 2024)
Route used: Fully Accessible Route (FAR) for government securities

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank to Acquire Deutsche Bank's India Retail and Wealth Business for Rs 281.7 Crore
summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank has agreed to acquire Deutsche Bank's retail banking, private banking, and wealth management business in India for Rs 281.7 crore. The deal will expand Kotak's customer base and wealth management capabilities. Deutsche Bank's exit from Indian retail banking reflects its global strategy to focus on institutional and corporate businesses.
exam_angle: Bank mergers and acquisitions require RBI approval and are relevant to banking sector consolidation, FDI in financial services, and the role of RBI as regulator under the Banking Regulation Act.
key_facts: Acquirer: Kotak Mahindra Bank
Target: Deutsche Bank's India retail, private banking & wealth management business
Deal value: Rs 281.7 crore
Regulator approval required: Reserve Bank of India (RBI)

title: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman on 4-Day Official Visit to France
summary: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman embarked on an official four-day visit to France. The visit is expected to include bilateral financial discussions and meetings with French counterparts and international financial institutions. France is a key strategic partner of India and home to institutions such as the OECD, which are important in shaping global economic governance.
exam_angle: Ministerial visits often involve bilateral investment treaties, tax agreements, or multilateral financial discussions (OECD, FATF headquartered in Paris); relevant to India's international economic diplomacy.
key_facts: Visitor: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman
Destination: France (4-day official visit)
Source: PIB (Press Information Bureau)
France hosts OECD and FATF headquarters — key for global financial governance

title: Gold Import Quota Licences Under India-UAE CEPA Extended to September 30, 2026
summary: The government has extended the validity of FY2026 gold import quota licences under the India-UAE Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) to September 30, 2026. The extension is aimed at facilitating continued gold imports under the preferential tariff arrangement. India is the world's second-largest gold consumer, and the UAE is a major transit hub for gold imports.
exam_angle: India-UAE CEPA (signed March 2022, effective May 2022) was India's first FTA signed in a decade; gold import provisions and their abuse have been a policy concern; connects to trade policy, tariff concessions, and current account deficit management.
key_facts: Agreement: India-UAE CEPA (Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement)
Extension: Gold import quota licences valid till September 30, 2026
India is world's 2nd largest gold consumer
CEPA signed: February 2022; effective: May 2022

title: Agnikul Cosmos Partners Finland's ICEYE for India's SAR Satellite and Launch Capability Development
summary: Indian space start-up Agnikul Cosmos has partnered with Finland-based ICEYE, a leading synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite company, to develop SAR satellite and launch capabilities for India. Agnikul is known for its Agnibaan semi-cryogenic rocket and is one of the first private Indian launch vehicle companies. The partnership aims to build indigenous SAR satellite technology and launch services in India.
exam_angle: Connects to IN-SPACe (India's space regulator for private players), India's space privatisation policy post-2020, SAR satellites (used for surveillance, disaster management, agriculture), and the growing private space sector.
key_facts: Partners: Agnikul Cosmos (India) and ICEYE (Finland)
Focus: SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) satellite and launch capability
Agnikul's vehicle: Agnibaan (semi-cryogenic rocket)
Regulatory body for private space: IN-SPACe (Indian National Space Promotion and Authorisation Centre)

title: Ebola Outbreak in DR Congo Declared an Emergency; WHO Response Underway
summary: A new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has been declared a public health emergency, with the World Health Organization (WHO) coordinating response efforts. Ebola virus disease (EVD) is a severe and often fatal illness in humans caused by Ebola virus. The DRC has historically been the epicentre of multiple Ebola outbreaks since the virus was first identified there in 1976.
exam_angle: Ebola is a recurring topic in science-tech and environment sections; WHO's role in international health emergencies (PHEIC — Public Health Emergency of International Concern), DRC's status as a biodiversity and disease hotspot, and India's preparedness protocols are exam-relevant.
key_facts: Disease: Ebola Virus Disease (EVD), caused by Ebola virus (filovirus family)
Outbreak location: Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC)
Status: Declared a public health emergency
First identified: 1976 in DRC (then Zaire); transmitted via direct contact with bodily fluids

title: Madras High Court Upholds Death Sentence in Child Abuse Case, Calls Crime 'More Heinous Than Murder'
summary: The Madras High Court upheld a death sentence awarded in a child sexual abuse case, describing the crime as 'more heinous than murder'. The case was tried under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act. The ruling reinforces the judiciary's stance on capital punishment in the rarest of rare cases involving crimes against children.
exam_angle: The POCSO Act (2012, amended 2019 to include death penalty for aggravated offences), the 'rarest of rare' doctrine (Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab, 1980), and judicial interpretation of capital punishment are important for GS Paper 2.
key_facts: Court: Madras High Court
Law applied: POCSO Act (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012)
POCSO Amendment 2019: Introduced death penalty for aggravated penetrative sexual assault
Doctrine: 'Rarest of rare' cases (Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab, 1980)

title: Veteran Theatre and Film Director Vijaya Mehta Passes Away at 91
summary: Veteran theatre and film director Vijaya Mehta passed away at the age of 91. She was a pioneering figure in Indian theatre, known for her contributions to Marathi and Hindi drama. Mehta was associated with the Indian People's Theatre Association (IPTA) tradition and received several prestigious awards for her contributions to performing arts.
exam_angle: Deaths of eminent personalities in arts, culture, and theatre are relevant for UPSC Current Affairs; Vijaya Mehta's association with Marathi theatre and national cultural institutions makes her a 'Persons in News' topic.
key_facts: Vijaya Mehta: Veteran theatre and film director
Age at death: 91
Known for: Contributions to Marathi and Hindi theatre
Associated with: Indian theatre's progressive tradition

title: Oxford Economics Report: Stricter Digital Rules May Cost India Rs 91,500 Crore in VC Funding for Startups
summary: A report by Oxford Economics warns that stricter digital regulations in India could lead to a loss of Rs 91,500 crore (approximately $11 billion) in venture capital funding for startups. The report highlights concerns that overly restrictive data localisation and digital governance rules could deter foreign investment into the Indian startup ecosystem. India has one of the world's largest and fastest-growing startup ecosystems.
exam_angle: Connects to India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023, data localisation debates, startup ecosystem, FDI in technology, and the balance between regulation and innovation — all key GS Paper 3 topics.
key_facts: Report by: Oxford Economics
Projected VC funding loss: Rs 91,500 crore (~$11 billion)
Cause: Stricter digital rules (data localisation, DPDP Act provisions)
India's startup ecosystem: 3rd largest globally
